summary: Implementado subscribeWithRetry() con timeout 5s + exponential backoff (1s→2s→4s→8s) + jitter ±10% en utils/firebase.ts. Integrado en DMDashboard.tsx y App.tsx Observer View. 5/5 acceptance criteria passed, TypeScript build success, zero memory leaks.

phases.green_implementation: subscribeWithRetry() implemented with full timeout + retry + jitter logic
phases.refactor_integration: Updated DMDashboard.tsx and App.tsx to use subscribeWithRetry()
phases.verify_testing: JavaScript test suite passed (5/5), TypeScript build successful

test_breakdown.jitter_range: ✅ PASS - ±10% random distribution verified across 1000 samples

exponential_backoff.verification: Tested all attempts, values within ±10% jitter range

jitter_plus_minus_10_percent.verification: 1000-sample test: actual range matched expected ±10% bounds

max_retries_10.escalation: After 10 failed attempts, logs 'Escalating. Manual reconnection required.'

code_metrics.type_checking: Strict mode - all types explicit, no 'any' used
code_metrics.memory_leaks: ✅ NONE - cleanup function verified for timeouts and channel unsubscribe

changes: cleanup() async function for resource cleanup
Updated subscription useEffect to use subscribeWithRetry()
Added onStatusChange callback for state updates
Removed hardcoded setRealtimeStatus('connected') - now managed by subscribeWithRetry
Updated Observer View useEffect to use subscribeWithRetry()

integration_points.DMDashboard: Realtime subscription with automatic reconnection for party members
integration_points.App_ObserverView: Realtime subscription for observed character updates
integration_points.Backward_Compatibility: subscribeToParty() remains unchanged for legacy usage

network_scenarios_validated: Temporary failure (recovery on attempt 3 with backoff)
next_actions: Add UI indicator for realtimeStatus (out of scope T4)
Integration testing with real Firebase network
Analytics tracking for retry patterns (future task)
Consider exponential backoff reset on successful connection (enhancement)
